sections.paragraph: f. only pl. צֵלָחֹות 2Ch 35:13, pans, such as were flat and broad, not deep. The primary idea is in shallow water which can easily be forded [but see צָלַח II .], (see צָלַח No. 1 ); whence Arab. زَاَحْلَحُ a shallow channel of a river, also زُلُحُ large pans, Eth. with the letters transposed ጻሕል፡ platter.
